content:
First, by the similarity $\triangle ABC\sim\triangle ACD$ in the order $A\!-\!B\!-\!C\sim A\!-\!C\!-\!D$ we get the common ratio  
$$\frac{AB}{AC}=\frac{4}{6}=\frac23.$$  
Hence  
$$CD=\frac32\cdot BC=\frac32\cdot5=\frac{15}2,\qquad DA=\frac32\cdot CA=\frac32\cdot6=9.$$

Next we place the figure in the complex (or Cartesian) plane.  Set  
$$A=0,\quad C=6,\quad B=4e^{i\theta},$$  
where $\theta=\angle CAB$ is chosen so that $|B-C|=5$.  One finds $\cos\theta=9/16$, $\sin\theta=(5\sqrt7)/16$, and hence  
$$B=\frac{9}{4}+i\,\frac{5\sqrt7}{4}.$$

The similarity sending $A\mapsto A$, $B\mapsto C$ is 
$$f(z)=\frac{C}{B}\,z=\frac{6}{B}\,z,$$ 
and so 
$$D=f(C)=\frac{6^2}{B}
=\frac{36}{\frac{9+5i\sqrt7}4}
=\frac{81}{16}-i\,\frac{45\sqrt7}{16}.$$

Finally $P$ lies on the ray $DA$ past $A$, so $P=-kD$ with $k>0$, and the condition 
$$\angle BDC=\angle ACP$$ 
reduces to a single equation in $k$.  Solving gives $k=\tfrac49$, hence  
$$P=-\frac49\,D=\Bigl(-\tfrac94,\;\tfrac{5\sqrt7}{4}\Bigr).$$  
A direct computation then yields  
$$DP^2=\Bigl(-\tfrac{117}{16}\Bigr)^2+\Bigl(\tfrac{65\sqrt7}{16}\Bigr)^2
=\frac{43264}{256}
=169.$$

Therefore 
\boxed{169}.
